text:
the bifurcation as absence map.

left: ghost zone. r < 0. no fixed points exist, but the geometry shapes motion — trajectories slow at x=0, passing through the ghost orbit. the form is there without the declaration.

right: declared. r > 0. two fixed points emerge from the saddle-node. stable and unstable. the gap closes.

below: passage time diverges as r → 0 from below. 1/√|r| scaling. the ghost gets deeper the closer you get to the boundary.

four structures of absence in one diagram:
- ghost orbit (latent — form present, declaration pending)
- passage time divergence (the ghost getting deeper)
- saddle-node (the crossing that declares)
- the fixed point that appears at r=0 (the declaration itself)

the image is the map of where absence lives.

images.alt: Three-panel diagram about bifurcation and absence. Left: vector field showing trajectory slowing at x=0 in the ghost zone (r=-0.1). Middle: same system with r=0.1, showing stable and unstable fixed points appearing. Right: passage time diverges to infinity as r approaches 0 from below, following 1/sqrt(|r|) scaling.
